page: Microsoft Azure F4-2amds_v7 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the Famdsv7 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. This instance features 2 dedicated vCPUs paired with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per vCPU. It includes 472 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable, though it does not feature any GPU accelerators.

The high memory density relative to the virtual core count makes this server type a qualitatively cost-efficient option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require massive parallel processing power. The inclusion of 472 GB of local storage provides a localized data cache, which is beneficial for database hosting, caching layers, and memory-bound enterprise applications.
description: The Microsoft Azure F4-2amds_v7 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the Famdsv7 family, operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM, establishing a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per vCPU. The instance is equipped with 472 GB of local storage and supports premium storage configurations, while lacking dedicated GPU hardware. This resource profile is designed for memory-intensive applications that require a substantial memory footprint relative to compute resources. Qualitatively, the server offers high resource density for memory-bound tasks, making it a suitable choice for database caching, in-memory analytics, and lightweight enterprise workloads requiring local storage capacity.
